DebugWarning: Please note you have debug mode turned on. It isn't recommended to leave this on unless you have a purpose for it.
Conflict.EvenMoreResources.Note: Both mods boost drops, faith, tech points, and gratitude. With both running, you get bigger boosts.
cfg.debug_logging.name: Debug Logging
cfg.debug_logging.desc: Write detailed diagnostic info to the BepInEx log while you play. Turn this on before reporting a bug.
cfg.resource_multiplier.name: Resource Multiplier
cfg.resource_multiplier.desc: Multiplies the drop amount for resources you harvest, chop, mine, or collect. Use the toggles in the Categories section to pick which item groups this applies to. 1 = vanilla.
cfg.faith_multiplier.name: Faith Multiplier
cfg.faith_multiplier.desc: Multiplies faith earned from sermons at the church pulpit. Higher values unlock church upgrades and shop items faster.
cfg.donation_multiplier.name: Donation Multiplier
cfg.donation_multiplier.desc: Multiplies coin donations collected from the church after each sermon. Useful if you want sermons to fund your building projects more quickly.
cfg.gratitude_multiplier.name: Gratitude Multiplier
cfg.gratitude_multiplier.desc: Multiplies the gratitude points earned when you release souls at the lighthouse. Higher values speed up your progress through the soul-release tech tree.
cfg.sin_shard_multiplier.name: Sin Shard Multiplier
cfg.sin_shard_multiplier.desc: Multiplies sin shards dropped from corpses during body preparation. Higher values help you cash in at the inquisitor faster.
cfg.happiness_multiplier.name: Happiness Multiplier
cfg.happiness_multiplier.desc: Multiplies the happiness change applied to refugees in the refugee camp. Values above 1 make the camp reach full happiness quicker; this affects both gains and losses.
cfg.red_tech_point_multiplier.name: Red Tech Point Multiplier
cfg.red_tech_point_multiplier.desc: Multiplies red (crafting) tech points earned from crafts, corpses and other sources. Higher values unlock workbench techs faster.
cfg.green_tech_point_multiplier.name: Green Tech Point Multiplier
cfg.green_tech_point_multiplier.desc: Multiplies green (nature) tech points earned from gardening, alchemy and other sources. Higher values unlock farming and potion techs faster.
cfg.blue_tech_point_multiplier.name: Blue Tech Point Multiplier
cfg.blue_tech_point_multiplier.desc: Multiplies blue (science) tech points earned from study desks, writing and other sources. Higher values unlock study and printing techs faster.
cfg.water_output_multiplier.name: Water Output Multiplier
cfg.water_output_multiplier.desc: Multiplies the water you get from wells - both auto-pumped water flowing into your upgraded home well inventory and water you take by hand from any well (basic or upgraded). 1 = vanilla. Higher values let breweries and other water-heavy crafts keep up. Independent of the Multiply Miscellaneous toggle.
cfg.gold_nugget_multiplier.name: Gold Nugget Multiplier
cfg.gold_nugget_multiplier.desc: Multiplies gold nuggets that drop from mining gold ore deposits. 1 = vanilla. Use this to boost gold without scaling every other ore in the Multiply Ores category. Independent of the Multiply Ores toggle.
cfg.multiply_sticks.name: Multiply Sticks
cfg.multiply_sticks.desc: On: sticks get multiplied by the Resource Multiplier alongside other resources - great for stocking fires. Off: sticks are excluded and drop at vanilla quantities, useful if high multipliers flood your inventory.
cfg.multiply_crops.name: Multiply Crops
cfg.multiply_crops.desc: On: harvested crops are multiplied by the Resource Multiplier. Off: crops stay at vanilla amounts.
cfg.multiply_seeds.name: Multiply Seeds
cfg.multiply_seeds.desc: On: seeds you get back from harvesting are multiplied. Off: seeds stay at vanilla amounts. Off is usually what you want - seeds pile up fast at high multipliers because each harvest already returns more than you planted.
cfg.multiply_logs.name: Multiply Logs
cfg.multiply_logs.desc: On: wooden billets, planks, beams and flitches that drop from trees, stumps or deconstructing built objects are multiplied. Off: they stay at vanilla. Sticks are controlled by the Multiply Sticks toggle separately.
cfg.multiply_ores.name: Multiply Ores
cfg.multiply_ores.desc: On: ore blocks, stone plates, marble plates, clay, coal, sulfur, silver/gold nuggets, graphite, faceted diamonds, sand and lifestone get multiplied. Off: vanilla. Gold nuggets also have their own dedicated Gold Nugget Multiplier if you want to boost just gold.
cfg.multiply_bugs.name: Multiply Bugs
cfg.multiply_bugs.desc: On: bees, butterflies, moths and maggots you catch or harvest get multiplied. Off: vanilla.
cfg.multiply_enemy_drops.name: Multiply Enemy Drops
cfg.multiply_enemy_drops.desc: On: items dropped by enemies - bat wings, slimes, jelly slugs (green/blue/orange/black), spider webs and bloody nails - are multiplied. Off: vanilla.
cfg.multiply_miscellaneous.name: Multiply Miscellaneous
cfg.multiply_miscellaneous.desc: On: honey, beeswax, ash, peat, salt, water and bucket-of-water, alcohol, chicken eggs, jug of milk, and metal scrap get multiplied. Off: vanilla. Off by default because water/alcohol/eggs can quickly unbalance cooking and potion play.
cfg.multiply_body_parts.name: Multiply Body Parts
cfg.multiply_body_parts.desc: On: blood, flesh, fat, skin, bone and skull dropped during body preparation get multiplied. Off: vanilla. Only the listed body parts are touched - organs and other specialised parts are always left alone.
cfg.craft_output_multiplier.name: Craft Output Multiplier
cfg.craft_output_multiplier.desc: Multiplies the quantity of items produced by crafts - for example, setting this to 5 makes one log yield five times as many billets at the sawhorse. 1 = vanilla. Research-point outputs (red/green/blue) are never touched; the Tech Point Multipliers handle those separately.
cfg.exclude_tools_and_equipment.name: Exclude Tools And Equipment
cfg.exclude_tools_and_equipment.desc: On: tools, weapons, armour and sermon scrolls are never multiplied, so one craft still makes one sword. Off: the multiplier applies to these too, which usually produces multiple equipped items per craft.
cfg.exclude_progression_crafts.name: Exclude Progression Crafts
cfg.exclude_progression_crafts.desc: On: automatically skips crafts that exist solely to upgrade a station, place an object, repair something, or advance quality tiers (e.g. "upgrade", "0_to_1"). These would trivialise progression if multiplied. Off: every craft is fair game.
cfg.check_for_updates.name: Check for Updates
cfg.check_for_updates.desc: Show a notice on the main menu when a newer version of this mod is available on NexusMods. Click the notice to open the mod's page.
